danger_signals.visual_form: Minimap indicators and unit sprites approaching player base
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat to player forces or structures; requires defensive response

danger_signals.visual_form: Energy/Metal indicators dropping into red zone on HUD
danger_signals.meaning: Economic collapse imminent; production and defensive systems will fail (nanostalling effect)

danger_signals.visual_form: Radar towers, metal extractors, and laser towers cease visual operation/glow
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of reconnaissance capability and defensive firepower; critical vulnerability state

danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened/greyed areas on minimap and main view without radar coverage
danger_signals.meaning: Unknown enemy activity; reconnaissance gap presents strategic risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, high-tech unit models (nuclear launchers, artillery, Core Krogoth mecha)
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent has achieved tech advantage; escalated threat level

safety_signals.visual_form: Energy/Metal indicators in green/positive range on HUD
safety_signals.meaning: Economy is stable; production can continue without slowdown

safety_signals.visual_form: Illuminated radar structures and visible minimap scout circles
safety_signals.meaning: Reconnaissance active; enemy approach will be detected

safety_signals.visual_form: High concentration of laser towers and fortifications around player base
safety_signals.meaning: Area is heavily defended; incoming attacks will face resistance

safety_signals.visual_form: Yellow backpack-equipped unit visible near base construction
safety_signals.meaning: Central irreplaceable unit is safe and assisting production; forces remain under direct control

safety_signals.visual_form: Increasingly advanced unit models in construction queues and on field
safety_signals.meaning: Player is advancing technologically; capability advantage emerging

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue/dark metallic (inferred from RTS convention and base screenshot reference)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Giant mecha unit 'Core Krogoth' serves as faction-specific superweapon signature
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Base structures and defensive installations with Core architectural style visible in screenshot

faction_indicators.color_identity: Gold/light contrasting (inferred from opposition to Core; Commander described with 'yellow backpack')
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Commander unit with distinctive yellow visual backpack marking serves as faction identifier
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player faction base layout and construction unit designs reflect Arm technology aesthetic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single Commander unit visible on map; small number of basic structures; limited unit variety in construction queues
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Commander performs construction duties; slow tech progression; focus on defensive positioning

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mid-game escalation (resource flow stable, tech advancement)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple unit types visible; upgraded structures; radar coverage expanding; tech-level indicators showing progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Production accelerates; offensive unit deployment begins; scouting and reconnaissance intensify

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: HUD shows red resource meters; production UI displays nanostalling indicator; structures glow dimly or cease operations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All construction and unit movement slows proportionally; power-dependent defenses fail; player forced into reactive survival mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Radar towers and laser towers become visually inactive/dark; energy meter at zero on HUD
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Radar reconnaissance ceases; defensive turrets stop firing; visibility collapses to immediate vicinity only

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High-tier units filling minimap (nuclear launchers, artillery, mecha); dense base infrastructure; tech level indicators maxed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Long-range bombardment visible across map; strategic positioning over raw unit count; attrition warfare dominates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy base structures disappear from minimap and main view; wreckage clusters visible where base was; no enemy unit sprites remaining
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Victory condition met; game concludes or player transitions to new objective

environmental_storytelling.example: Destroyed unit and structure remnants left behind accumulate on map, creating visual history of combat encounters; wreckage can be reclaimed for metal, incentivizing players to move through battle zones
Metal deposits and energy-producing organic structures scattered across map geography; distribution tells story of resource scarcity and strategic chokepoints where control is critical
Fog of war creates visual darkness in unexplored areas; radar towers illuminate strategic regions; contrast between visible and hidden space communicates information advantage

environmental_storytelling.technique: Base density as narrative of escalation
environmental_storytelling.example: Player base grows from single Commander to sprawling industrial complex; visual expansion of defensive infrastructure and construction facilities mirrors the narrative progression from survival to dominance

Relative size of advanced units (Core Krogoth, nuclear launchers) compared to basic infantry communicates technological hierarchy and power differential; their positioning on map indicates threat concentration

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Visible near structures being built; moves to new construction sites in queue; presence signals active production and base development
Becomes invisible to enemy scouts; tactical repositioning or escaping threat; when cloaked, absence from minimap indicates evasion strategy
Emits visual glow/activation indicator; minimap shows active coverage radius; enemy units within range trigger detection alerts
Glow extinguishes; coverage disappears from minimap; reconnaissance blind spot created; strategic vulnerability exposed
Continuously harvests nearby metal deposits; visible animation; resource stream flows to player reserves
Animation stops; no resource accumulation despite proximity to deposits; player forced to rely on wreckage reclamation
Tracks and fires on approaching enemy units; visible beam effects; active defense posture
Ceases tracking and firing; stands inert; defensive capability nullified; base becomes vulnerable
Completes units in queue; new unit sprites spawn from factory; production UI shows build progress animation
Build progress slows visibly; unit completion takes extended time; factory operations visibly stutter/hesitate in animation
Executes queued movement commands automatically; patrol routes visible through movement trails; minimizes player micromanagement load
Massive visual presence; distinctive silhouette; long build times reflected in late-game appearance; signals opponent tech dominance if enemy-controlled

notes: Total Annihilation's visual communication relies heavily on economic state translation to defensive/offensive capability shifts. The game's streaming resource model means danger and safety are directly tied to HUD meter positions rather than discrete events. Faction distinction appears minimal in available source material; both Core and Arm likely use standard RTS color opposition (Blue vs. Yellow/Gold). The Commander's yellow backpack serves as player faction identifier. Minimap fog of war and radar coverage create spatial storytelling about information advantage. Unit scale (basic infantry vs. Krogoth mecha) communicates tech progression visually. Environmental wreckage accumulation creates visual palimpsest of prior battles, reinforcing RTS resource-scarcity gameplay through aesthetic persistence.
